steps.title: Set up the inequality
steps.content:
Four consecutive odd integers from least to greatest: $x,\ x+2,\ x+4,\ x+6$. Third = $x+4$; fourth = $x+6$.

"$28 \cdot$ third $\le$ (sum of first and fourth) $- 50$":
$$28(x+4) \le (x + (x+6)) - 50$$

steps.title: Solve for the greatest $x$
steps.content:
$$28x + 112 \le 2x + 6 - 50$$
$$28x + 112 \le 2x - 44$$
$$26x \le -156$$
$$x \le -6$$
The greatest odd integer satisfying $x \le -6$ is $x = -7$.

The answer is -7.
